mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of 2009-09-15 indicates zero-bound policy
regime_hint: post-shock monetary emergency, zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-bound regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE falling > 0.2 percentage points) signals labor market recovery and rising consumer confidence. This improvement in employment conditions drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8% as of 2009-09-15, elevated but potentially declining from crisis peak
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1% or t-test p > 0.10) in periods following UNRATE declines > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above trend, indicating consumer spending recovery and rising aggregate demand. This demand signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings revisions incorporate stronger consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,688M as of 2009-09-15, recovering from crisis lows
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following RSAFS growth > 0.5% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.8% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates month-over-month (> 0.4% MoM), signaling capacity utilization recovery and manufacturing strength. This supply-side improvement predicts positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.44 as of 2009-08-15, recovering from 2008-2009 crisis trough
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following INDPRO growth > 0.4% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3% or t-test p > 0.20), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously (each > 0.3% MoM), indicating broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumption channels. This joint demand signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 0.3% MoM AND RSAFS > 0.3% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598K units and RSAFS at 344,688M as of 2009-08-15 and 2009-09-15 respectively
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns when both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate > 0.3% MoM are not significantly positive (mean < 1.0% or t-test p > 0.12), or interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 0.2% MoM while unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously declines, signaling strong labor market momentum. This dual labor-market confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as consumer income and confidence strengthen.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.2% MoM AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,947K and UNRATE at 9.8% as of 2009-09-15
regime_hint: synchronized labor market recovery with employment gains and jobless rate decline
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns when both PAYEMS and UNRATE signals trigger are not significantly positive (mean < 2.0% or t-test p > 0.10), or interaction effect is weaker than either signal in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued (YoY growth < 2.5%) while monetary policy is accommodative (FEDFUNDS < 1%), creating a regime where real rates are deeply negative. This regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets anticipate eventual policy normalization and inflation surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.79 (low inflation) and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% (deep accommodation) as of 2009-09-15
regime_hint: post-shock deflation risk with emergency monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in this regime are significantly positive (mean > 0.5% or t-test p < 0.10), or the interaction of low inflation and low rates fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is recovering from crisis lows (quarter-over-quarter growth > 0.5% annualized), signaling end of recession. In this early-recovery regime, S&P 500 returns are positive over 20 trading days as earnings expectations reset upward and risk-on sentiment dominates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,901.5B (2017 dollars) as of 2009-Q2, recovering from 2008-2009 recession trough
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns during periods of positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (mean < 1.2% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in recovery regimes.
